title: Inventory satellite ash-detection products, volcanic monitoring networks, ash-transport models, and aviation operational data sources
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite ash-detection products (MODIS, VIIRS, infrared sounders), volcanic monitoring networks (VAACs, ground stations, seismic), ash-transport models (HYSPLIT, NAME, FLEXPART), and aviation encounter data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit regional coverage assessment.
inputs: ICAO VAAC coverage documentation and detection capability reports
Satellite ash-detection product documentation and validation studies
Ash-transport model documentation and intercomparison studies
Volcanic monitoring network density data by region
Aviation ash encounter case studies and engine damage data
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, detection capability, and documented regional gaps — particularly false-positive rates, nighttime limitations, and submarine volcanic monitoring coverage.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering detection capability, regional coverage, false-positive documentation, and monitoring-network density.

title: Map volcanic ash detection capability and monitoring-network gaps for at least two volcanic regions
outcome: A regional ash-detection capability analysis for at least two volcanic regions, documenting satellite detection accuracy, ground-monitor density, VAAC operational capacity, and false-positive/false-negative risk assessment.
inputs: ICAO VAAC coverage and capability data
Satellite ash-detection validation studies for target regions
Historical eruption and ash-plume data for target regions
expected_artifact: validation.md update with detection-capability methodology and regional results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms VAAC capability data extraction, satellite detection accuracy assessment, and monitoring-network gap documentation for each target region.
done_condition: Detection-capability analysis includes methodology, regional results, false-positive and false-negative documentation, monitoring-network gaps, and replication status for at least two regions.

title: Analyze ash-concentration forecast uncertainty from eruption-source parameter propagation
outcome: A forecast-uncertainty analysis documenting how eruption-source parameter uncertainty (ash height, mass loading, particle size) propagates through ash-transport models into concentration-forecast uncertainty at aviation-route waypoints.
inputs: ash-transport model documentation and sensitivity studies
aviation route waypoint data for target volcanic regions
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with forecast-uncertainty methodology and waypoint-level results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms source-parameter sensitivity analysis, uncertainty propagation methodology, and waypoint-level results documentation.
done_condition: Forecast-uncertainty analysis includes methodology, source-parameter sensitivity results, waypoint-level uncertainty bounds, and replication status.

title: Red-team ash-detection overstatement and false-negative safety risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how ash-detection capability maps could overstate coverage, how false-positive contamination could undermine operational trust, and how false-negative missed detections in poorly monitored regions could create catastrophic aviation safety risks.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every detection-capability claim has false-positive and false-negative documentation, every forecast claim has source-parameter uncertainty, and every output has explicit aviation-safety non-use cases. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every detection and forecast claim is either blocked, softened to uncertainty-limited language with explicit false-negative risk documentation, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match aviation safety authority and airline operations decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the detection-capability and forecast-uncertainty analysis would change any real decision for aviation safety authorities, airline operations centers, or VAACs without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for aviation safety and airline operations audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that detection-capability maps are used to justify airspace closures in well-monitored regions while ignoring gaps in poorly monitored regions, or that forecast uncertainty is presented as definitive guidance. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., London VAAC operations officer), named decision (e.g., ash-advisory confidence level for route planning), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for airspace closure recommendations, not for safe-flight threshold determination), and replicated field-reality review.
